app.title: VizVerso
app.tagline: Analyze Tableau workbooks and visualize dependencies.
app.description: Drop a .twbx or .twb file to visualize dashboard structures and calculated field dependencies in detail.

safe_analysis.title: Safe Analysis
safe_analysis.desc: Analysis data is never sent to any server.

error.default: An error occurred while parsing the file

detail.layout_hint: Click a chart to open its detail
detail.layout_empty: No layout information available for this dashboard.
detail.label: Label

about.title: About VizVerso
about.description: A minimal analysis tool to understand the structure of Tableau workbooks.
about.privacy_desc: This tool operates solely on the client side; workbook analysis data is never sent to external servers. We collect anonymous usage statistics via Cloudflare Analytics for service improvement purposes.
about.as_is_desc: This tool is provided 'as is'. The author takes no responsibility for any direct or indirect damages (data corruption, impact on business, etc.) caused by using this tool.
about.relationship_desc: This is a personal project and is not affiliated with Salesforce.com, Inc. or Tableau Software, LLC.

dropzone.title: Drop Tableau Workbook
dropzone.hint: or click to select a file (.twbx, .twb)
dropzone.try_sample_source: Sample data source: Tableau official Superstore dashboard

handover.title: Handover
handover.situation: You inherited a workbook with no documentation
handover.solution: Grasp its structure and calculation logic in minutes

cleanup.title: Cleanup
cleanup.situation: You want to tidy up a workbook that has grown heavy
cleanup.solution: Spot removable calculated fields with unused-field detection

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.situation: Writing design docs takes too long
documentation.solution: Export the full definition list to Excel in one click

q1.q: Is the data in my uploaded workbook safe?
q1.a: All analysis runs entirely in your browser, and the contents of your workbook are never sent to any server.

q2.a: Both .twbx (packaged workbooks) and .twb (XML) are supported, up to a file size of 100MB.

q3.a: Yes, it is free to use. Donations to support continued development (Buy Me a Coffee) are welcome.

q4.q: Do I need a Tableau license or installation?
q4.a: No. It runs entirely in your browser.

guide.title: How to Read This Tableau Analysis
guide.intro: This file is the analyzed structure of a Tableau workbook (.twbx / .twb). Each sheet covers a different aspect. The header row is frozen and filterable, so you can sort and narrow down.
guide.desc_objects: Each object on a dashboard (sheet/text/parameter/image): type, mark type, current value, and layout coordinates.
guide.desc_shelf: Fields placed on each worksheet's shelves (columns/rows/filters).
guide.desc_parameters: List of parameters: current value, allowed values, configuration.
guide.desc_fields: All fields: data type, role, calc type, usage, and formula.
guide.desc_field_dependencies: Other fields referenced by calculated fields (dependencies).
guide.desc_worksheet: (Sheets named after each worksheet) Mark type and encoding (color/size/label, etc.) details.
guide.tip_id: The "ID" column is shared across all sheets. Match the same ID across sheets to trace how a field is used.
guide.tip_coords: Coordinates X / Y / Width / Height are pixel values scaled to the dashboard's actual size (origin at top-left). e.g. a 1300×800 dashboard spans Width=1300, Height=800. If the dashboard has no fixed size, values fall back to Tableau normalized units (whole dashboard = 100000 × 100000).
guide.tip_filter: The header row is frozen and filterable. Use the ▼ on each header to sort and filter.
guide.legend_unused: Unused field (not referenced by any sheet or calculation)

usage.unused_hint: Not used by any sheet or calculation

restore.hint: Reopen the file you were analyzing before reloading

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.stripe_link_note: For details, please see Stripe's official website.

data_security.title: Data Security
data_security.content: VizVerso never sends the contents of uploaded Tableau workbooks (.twbx, .twb) to any server. All analysis runs inside your browser, and your data never leaves it.

information_collect.title: Information Collection
information_collect.content:
The following information may be collected by this service:
- Name and email address provided during inquiries.
- Payment information for Stripe transactions (card details are managed securely by Stripe and are not stored by VizVerso).
- Anonymized access logs for service quality improvement (Cloudflare Web Analytics).

purpose.title: Purpose of Use
purpose.content: The collected information will only be used for providing services, responding to inquiries, and improving the quality of the service. We will not provide information to third parties except when required by law.

analytics.title: Anonymous Usage Statistics
analytics.content: To improve the service, we may collect anonymous usage events (such as which features are used and how often). These statistics never include the contents of your files, file names, formulas, or any personal information. We do not use cookies and we respect your browser's Do Not Track setting.

stripe.title: Payment Processing
stripe.content: We use services from Stripe, Inc. for payment processing. Your payment information is protected and managed according to Stripe's Privacy Policy.

graph.title: Dependency Graph
graph.hint: Click to expand/collapse; use the ◎ button to center
graph.truncated: Some nodes are omitted due to graph size
graph.relayout_hint: Recompute the layout (node positions will be reset)

step_upload.title: Drop your workbook
step_upload.desc: Just drag & drop a .twb / .twbx file to start the analysis. No file at hand? Use "Try with a sample" to explore a demo.

step_privacy.title: Your data never leaves the browser
step_privacy.desc: All analysis runs entirely in your browser. Your workbook is never uploaded to any server.

step_explore.title: Explore structure & calculations
step_explore.desc: Switch between dashboards and sheets in the left navigator. Click a calculated field to trace its references in the detail drawer and dependency graph.

step_health.title: Check workbook health
step_health.desc: Right after analysis, the Health Check view opens first. It automatically detects nested LODs, duplicated formulas, unused fields, and more, then sums everything up in an overall health score.

step_export.title: Export to Excel
step_export.desc: Export the analysis results to an Excel file in one click. You can reopen this guide anytime from the ? button in the header.

health.title: Workbook Health Check
health.subtitle: Automatically checks calculated fields for performance, complexity, and cleanup opportunities.
health.no_findings_desc: All checked calculated fields passed the rules.

nestedLod.title: Nested LOD expressions
nestedLod.desc: An LOD expression contains another LOD expression. This complicates queries and can hurt performance.

countd.title: COUNTD usage
countd.desc: COUNTD is an expensive aggregation, especially on file-based data sources.

heavyStringCalc.title: Row-level string processing
heavyStringCalc.desc: String functions like CONTAINS and REGEXP are evaluated per row and can be slow on large data.

deepIfChain.title: Long conditional chain
deepIfChain.desc: Too many IF/ELSEIF or CASE branches. Consider groups, sets, or a lookup table instead.

deepDependency.title: Deep dependency chain
deepDependency.desc: Calculated field references are deeply chained, making logic hard to trace. Consider consolidating intermediate fields.

duplicateFormula.title: Duplicated formulas
duplicateFormula.desc: Multiple fields share the same formula. Consolidating them improves maintainability.

unusedCalc.title: Unused calculated fields
unusedCalc.desc: Calculated fields not referenced by any sheet, calculation, or dashboard. Removing them slims down the workbook.

unusedParam.title: Unused parameters
unusedParam.desc: Parameters that are not referenced anywhere.

diff.title: Workbook Comparison
diff.subtitle: Load a before and after workbook to see added, removed, and changed elements at a glance.
